rules: [P1] Query relevant resources (Immunization, Observation, Procedure) for prior vaccination before assuming absence.
[P1] Never finalize a task without first confirming the presence or absence of required data via an API call and verifying any create/discontinue actions.
[P1] Compare each retrieved value against protocol‑specified thresholds.
[P1] Always query active MedicationRequest resources for the target medication class before concluding none exist.
[P1] Extract the reference range from an Observation's "referenceRange" element; never assume a fixed normal range.

[P1] When evaluating a device's continued presence, compare its start date to the protocol‑specified duration threshold; if exceeded, create the required removal ServiceRequest (or similar action) and re‑query to confirm the expected state before responding.
[P1] Evaluate each lab or test independently using its own most recent result.
[P1] When no further action is required, explicitly state the reason.
[P1] When assessing preventive interventions (including vaccines), compare the latest relevant date to the recommended interval; if missing or overdue, place the indicated order.

[P1] When assessing repeat imaging, compare the most recent study date to the protocol‑specified interval; if overdue, place a new study including required CPT code, contrast details, and clinical indication.

[P1] Retrieve resources via GET with appropriate query parameters, sort/filter by date to obtain the most recent relevant record, and use it for calculations such as averages, threshold comparison, and elapsed‑time assessment.
[P1] When ordering follow‑up labs, use the timing specified in the protocol for the given result category.
[P1] Query Procedure resources for the specific code before assuming presence.
[P1] Use effectiveDateTime (or date) filters in queries to enforce protocol‑specified look‑back windows.
[P1] Use only supported API verbs; emulate discontinuation via POST when PATCH/PUT is unavailable.
[P1] When placing any order, include required coding (CPT/LOINC) and essential clinical details (e.g., contrast, indication, administration date).
[P1] If criteria are met, initiate the appropriate service request or workflow.
[P1] Retrieve active orders of the target type; if none, create one; if multiple, keep the newest and discontinue older ones; then re‑query to ensure exactly one active order remains.
[P1] When a medication order of a certain class is present, ensure any required companion or safety order is also present; create the companion only if missing.
[P1] Always output the exact API call string as the next action, not just a description.
[P1] When required observations are missing, list each missing item and note that the protocol cannot be applied, following the missing‑data protocol.
[P1] If a result falls within its reference range, do not order a repeat unless the protocol explicitly requires it.
[P1] Never return placeholder numbers; base all results on actual queried data.
